headline: How The Story Is Shifting For Yum Brands (YUM) As Street Targets Converge Around US$170
summary: The blended fair value estimate for Yum! Brands now sits at about US$171.92, a very small move from roughly US$171.75, keeping the headline target effectively stable. Behind that, several firms, including JPMorgan with its US$170 price target, are refreshing their work around growth quality and execution, as well as how durable the business model looks in their scenarios. As you read on, you will see how to track these shifts in the story and what to watch as the analyst narrative continues...

content.title: How The Story Is Shifting For Yum Brands (YUM) As Street Targets Converge Around US$170
content.summary: The blended fair value estimate for Yum! Brands now sits at about US$171.92, a very small move from roughly US$171.75, keeping the headline target effectively stable. Behind that, several firms, including JPMorgan with its US$170 price target, are refreshing their work around growth quality and execution, as well as how durable the business model looks in their scenarios. As you read on, you will see how to track these shifts in the story and what to watch as the analyst narrative continues...

headline: APO, KKR, OWL Confront $20B Private-Credit Pullout Demands
summary: The scale of the exit rush that rocked private credit funds during the first quarter is now known. According to calculations by the Financial Times, investors attempted to yank more than $20 billion from such funds during the period, as worries mounted over lending to the software industry, a sector hit hardest by recent jitters that AI could render them obsolete. Several industry titans were impacted by this dash for the exits, including Blackstone (BX), KKR (KKR), Apollo Global Management (APO

headline: JPMorgan Announces Cash Distributions for the JPMorgan ETFs
summary: TORONTO, April 10, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- J. P. Morgan Asset Management (JPMAM)* today announced the final March 2026 cash distributions for the below listed JPMorgan ETFs. The JPMorgan ETFs trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX). Unitholders of record on April 17, 2026 will receive cash distributions payable on April 23, 2026. Details of the “per unit” distributions are as follows: JPMorgan ETF nameTicker symbolDistribution per unit ($)Payment frequencyJPMorgan US Ultra-Short Income Active

headline: JPMorgan Cuts Nutanix to Neutral With a $44 Target: The Cloud Infrastructure Rally May Be Cooling Off
summary: Nutanix (NASDAQ:NTNX) stock got the cold shoulder from JPMorgan on Friday, as the firm cut its rating from Overweight to Neutral and set a price target of $44. The downgrade lands as Nutanix shares have already lost significant ground, suggesting Wall Street is reassessing whether the cloud infrastructure rally has run its course for software-layer ... JPMorgan Cuts Nutanix to Neutral With a $44 Target: The Cloud Infrastructure Rally May Be Cooling Off

headline: Anthropic Has Banks, Regulators Rattled Over AI Cyber Risks. Some Big Names Could Benefit.
summary: Cybersecurity stocks could be set for a massive boost from risks tied to Anthropic’s latest advances in artificial intelligence, which have stoked concerns in Washington and on Wall Street that a new generation of hackers could test the defenses of the country’s biggest banks.  Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, as well as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, arranged an emergency meeting of top bank bosses earlier this week, Bloomberg reported on Friday, to address the risks raised by the limited release of Anthropic’s newest AI model, known as Claude Mythos.  Each of the lenders invited, according to the report, are part of the so-called Big 8 group of Global Systemically Important Banks, which the Fed deems crucial to broader financial system risks and imposes higher capital buffer rules as backstop.

headline: Goldman’s ‘Unwavering’ Support Shows Gulf’s Lure for Wall Street
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Late last month, Blackstone Inc. announced the first inbound Gulf private equity deal since Iran started attacking Middle Eastern hubs, while Citigroup Inc.’s top boss fired off a 600-word memo underlining the bank’s enthusiasm for its business in the region.Blackstone’s $250 million commitment, hailed as a “masterstroke” by an executive at a rival firm, and Jane Fraser’s note came just as the United Arab Emirates’ carefully-cultivated image of safety and stability was facing a te

headline: Demand for Dollars in Swaps Ebbs Alongside Global Risks
summary: (Bloomberg) -- By at least one metric in the $9.5 trillion foreign-exchange markets, demand for the dollar is ebbing amid the tenuous ceasefire between the US and Iran.Measures of the so-called cross-currency basis — the extra cost investors pay or receive when sourcing dollars overseas instead of the US — show a steady waning in appetite for the greenback in recent days, particularly against the euro and Swiss franc.“This is a simple reversal of the dollar trade,” said Nathan Thooft, a senior p

headline: JPMorgan buys 60,000 metric tons of biomass-based carbon removals
summary: The purchase is part of a 10-year deal with carbon removal company Graphyte, which uses a process it calls “carbon casting” to turn biomass waste into carbon rich cubes to sequester underground.

headline: Not JPMorgan, Not Bank of America — SoFi Tops Forbes Best Banks List In The US
summary: It's not JP Morgan Chase & Co. It's not Bank of America Corp. According to Forbes' latest rankings, the top bank in the U.S. is SoFi Technologies, Inc. —a fintech-turned-bank that's still trading far from its highs. The recognition, based on a global survey of over 54,000 consumers, puts SoFi ahead of incumbents like Citigroup, Inc. and American Express Co. But while the headline signals a shift in customer perception, the stock tells a more cautious story. Strong Brand, Weak Tape SoFi stock has

headline: Anthropic’s New AI Model Triggered an Emergency Banking Meeting. It’s a Reason to Buy Cybersecurity Stocks.
summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, as well as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, arranged an emergency meeting of top bank bosses earlier this week, Bloomberg reported on Friday, to address the risks raised by the limited release of Anthropic’s newest AI model, known as Claude Mythos.  Each of the lenders invited, according to the report, are part of the so-called Big 8 group of Global Systemically Important Banks, which the Fed deems crucial to broader financial system risks and imposes higher capital buffer rules as backstop.  The concern stems from the ability of Mythos to find and exploit so-called zero-day vulnerabilities in tech infrastructure, based on relatively simple prompting.

content.title: Anthropic’s New AI Model Triggered an Emergency Banking Meeting. It’s a Reason to Buy Cybersecurity Stocks.
content.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, as well as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, arranged an emergency meeting of top bank bosses earlier this week, Bloomberg reported on Friday, to address the risks raised by the limited release of Anthropic’s newest AI model, known as Claude Mythos.  Each of the lenders invited, according to the report, are part of the so-called Big 8 group of Global Systemically Important Banks, which the Fed deems crucial to broader financial system risks and imposes higher capital buffer rules as backstop.  The concern stems from the ability of Mythos to find and exploit so-called zero-day vulnerabilities in tech infrastructure, based on relatively simple prompting.

headline: Wall Street Banks Try Out Anthropic’s Mythos as US Urges Testing
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Wall Street banks are starting to test Anthropic PBC’s Mythos model internally as Trump administration officials encourage them to use it to detect vulnerabilities.While JPMorgan Chase & Co. was the only bank named as part of an initiative to test the Mythos model, other major financial institutions have also gained access or expect to in the coming days, according to people familiar with the matter. Goldman Sachs Group Inc., Citigroup Inc., Bank of America Corp. and Morgan Stanle

headline: Why The Narrative Around Argan (AGX) Is Shifting With AI Power Demand And Valuation Risks
summary: Argan’s fair value estimate has been reset from US$373.80 to US$473.20, which puts a brighter spotlight on how analysts are framing the story around power demand tied to AI and data centers. Recent commentary from firms such as JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs connects this higher target range with expectations for Argan’s project pipeline and the company’s role in firm generation capacity. As you read on, you will see how to track these shifts in the analyst narrative and what they might mean for...

headline: Q1 Earnings Season to Gain Momentum: What Will it Show?
